mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market expansion that increases consumer purchasing power and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 136133k as of Aug 2013 shows ongoing recovery momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of months where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows month-over-month growth, real rates turn negative, incentivizing equity allocation over cash and driving positive forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of Sep 2013
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with positive inflation
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window when trigger is active, or 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 850k units signals weakening demand-side momentum and reduced construction employment multipliers, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 45% of months meeting the trigger condition, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) remains above 7.0%, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market slack that constrains profit margins and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and UNRATE at 7.3% as of Aug 2013
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month, indicating strengthening consumer demand that translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in < 58% of months where RSAFS growth exceeds 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.2 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window while payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains positive, signaling accelerating labor market tightening that raises wage pressures and equity risk premiums, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month decline > 0.2pp AND PAYEMS month-over-month growth > 0%
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.3% and PAYEMS at 136133k as of Aug 2013
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in < 50% of triggered periods, or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2.0% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 0.15%, the Fed faces conflicting signals that increase policy uncertainty and equity volatility, producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15681B as of Apr 2013, FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of Aug 2013
regime_hint: monetary policy normalization with growth uncertainty
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 48% of triggered instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.0% year-over-year, signaling productivity-driven growth without demand-pull inflation that supports equity valuations and produces positive 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.9462 and CPIAUCSL at 233.318 as of Jul-Aug 2013
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 56% of triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.
